testers.focus: Phase-3 preflight design review: long-only compliance + methodological/lockbox leakage, verified against the actual repo (engine/backtester.py, auto_evolve.py, benchmark/data_lockbox.py, benchmark/spec.py, mirofish/scenario_engine.py, engine/features.py, models/*meta.json).
blocking_issues: LOCKBOX LEAKAGE VIA PRETRAINED MODELS (HARD BLOCKER): models/lstm_*_training_meta.json show trained_at=2026-03-29 on train_candles=51194 (1h) ≈ 5.8 years, i.e. ~mid-2020 -> 2026-03-29. That training window runs straight THROUGH the sealed lockbox (2024-06-07 -> 2026-06-07). Any strategy consuming these LSTM/cluster artifacts (lstm_pattern, plus any cluster_variant feature) is contaminated with lockbox data. The plan only schedules an 'lstm provenance audit' as a doubtful-tier polish in iteration 7. This must be a Phase-3-wide gate: NO model trained past 2024-06-07 may be loaded by ANY evolved/evaluated strategy. lstm_pattern and cluster-derived features must be excluded until the models are retrained with a hard train-end cutoff < lockbox_cutoff_ts, else the whole portfolio's OOS read is invalid.

FUNDING=0 IS NOT WIRED THROUGH THE TWO PLACES THAT MATTER: funding_bps_per_8h is a Backtester.__init__ argument (default 1.0 = perp cost), NOT a field of BacktestConfig. fitness_worker builds Backtester(bt_config, risk_config) (auto_evolve.py ~line 526) with no funding arg, and WalkForwardValidator builds its OWN internal Backtester(config, risk_config) at engine/backtester.py line 512 with no funding arg. So setting a config value does nothing; both call sites (and sweep/run.py + benchmark/runner) must explicitly pass funding_bps_per_8h=0.0. The plan says 'everywhere' but never names the WF-validator internal Backtester (line 512) — if missed, every walk-forward fold silently charges perp funding on spot, breaking spot-cost honesty.

LONG_ONLY FLAG MUST ALSO THREAD INTO THE WF VALIDATOR'S INTERNAL BACKTESTER: the plan's enforcement layer 1 adds long_only to Backtester and builds Backtester(..., long_only=True) 'in evolution, sweep/run, benchmark', but the GA's selection objective (is_train_sharpe/is_val_sharpe) comes from WalkForwardValidator, which constructs Backtester internally at line 512. If long_only is not plumbed through WalkForwardValidator.__init__ -> its internal Backtester, the GA could SELECT genomes on fold metrics that still include short trades, even though the reported full-sample backtest is long-only. This is a subtle selection-time long-only breach. Wrapper-filter (layer 2) does mitigate it IF the wrapper is shared by the validator path — verify the validator uses the same strategy_fn closure (it does, it is passed in), so the wrapper filter is the real safety net; still, add the engine flag to the validator too for defense-in-depth.

WRAPPER REWRITE MUST PRESERVE THE 3-ARG SIGNATURE: the live wrapper at auto_evolve.py:519-523 is def strategy_fn(d,i,p) and the Backtester calls strategy_fn(df,i,None)/strategy_fn(df,i,position) with exactly 3 positional args. The plan's rewrite shows def strategy_fn(d,i,p): sig = strategy_fn_ref(d,i,p,regime=...) — that is fine, but the plan text elsewhere implies signature strategy_fn(df,bar_idx,position,regime); if anyone implements the wrapper with 4 params or drops p, the backtester call will raise. Lock the wrapper to (d,i,p) and pass regime internally via _bar_regime(d,i).
recommendations: Add a global model-provenance gate BEFORE iteration 2, not iteration 7: load every model meta, assert max training timestamp/last candle < lockbox_cutoff_ts (2024-06-07). Make load of any post-cutoff model a hard error (-999 fitness) for any strategy that touches it. Retrain LSTM/cluster artifacts with an explicit train-end < 2024-06-07 and record train_end_ts in the meta, or drop lstm_pattern and all cluster_variant features from Phase 3 entirely.

In iteration 1, explicitly pass funding_bps_per_8h=0.0 AND long_only=True to: (a) fitness_worker's Backtester (auto_evolve.py ~526), (b) WalkForwardValidator.__init__ so its internal Backtester at engine/backtester.py:512 inherits both, (c) sweep/run.py, (d) benchmark/runner. Add an assertion in the smoke test that sum(trades_df.funding) == 0 and that no fold's metrics derive from funding.

Promote lockbox_days from 120 to 730 in benchmark/spec.py (BenchmarkSpec default is 120 and snapshots/manifest.json currently says 120) AND rebuild the frozen snapshot (build_snapshots / benchmark __main__) so lockbox_cutoff_ts is recomputed to 2024-06-07. Verify get_evolution_window(BENCHMARK_SPEC) returns only timestamp < cutoff; the smoke test should print min/max timestamps of the evolution window and assert max < 2024-06-07. NOTE: the lockbox accessor is DataLockbox.in_sample(), not in_sample_view() as the plan's md names it — correct the doc to avoid a wrong-method implementation.

Add the ordering-repair blocks the plan promises (fast<slow, tenkan<kijun, ema_fast<ema_slow) — today only TP>SL is repaired (auto_evolve.py ~410/438). Mirror that block for each ordered pair, and add the unit test asserting every STRATEGY_REGISTRY entry has the full SHARED_LONG_CORE keys and ZERO forbidden short-only keys (confirmed present today: rsi_short_max:127, contraction_rsi_overbought:229, rsi_overbought:262, etc.). Make the test fail CI if any short-only key survives.

Audit DSR n_trials accounting across the full-re-evolution: the plan re-evolves every strategy from scratch, which massively inflates the real cumulative trial count. Confirm prior_trials + total_evaluated keeps accumulating across all 8 iterations (it appears to via n_trials_now = prior_trials + total_evaluated + 1) so DSR stays honestly deflated; reset would understate selection risk. Document the cumulative n_trials carried into the final sealed-OOS read.

Enforce the 'old evolved PARAMS may seed at most ONE genome' rule mechanically, not by convention — those values were tuned with shorts and across the (then-120d) lockbox tail. Best practice: seed zero short-era genomes for any strategy whose edge was the short branch (donchian Sharpe 2.79, ichimoku 2.28, vwap, connors, obv short). Re-evolve purely from random_genome on the in_sample() view.

Single-read discipline for the sealed OOS: gate the iteration-8 lockbox evaluation behind a one-shot guard (e.g. a written 'lockbox_consumed' marker) so a re-run cannot silently re-tune against it. Reports must record the manifest_hash/fingerprint and the cumulative n_trials at read time for reproducibility.

testers.focus: Phase-3 preflight design review: genome breadth, search bounds, no last-2-year optimization, param specificity, long-only and lockbox integrity — verified against the actual repo.
blocking_issues: GENOME-BREADTH / IMPLEMENTABILITY COLLISION: stop_loss_atr_mult and take_profit_atr_mult are hard-coded INLINE in ~23 places across the existing param_space (and PARAMS) of nearly every strategy in auto_evolve.py (e.g. vol_regime_arb lines 228-229). adx_min appears inline 8x, volume_threshold 5x, rsi_overbought/oversold 2x each. The plan says SHARED_LONG_CORE is 'merged into every STRATEGY_REGISTRY[name][param_space] via a build helper' and that Layer-B blocks must contain NO risk/exec/gate keys, but it only explicitly enumerates DELETION of short-only keys. It never states the helper will first STRIP these duplicated inline exec/gate keys. If the merge runs as written, either (a) the inline keys silently shadow/collide with the shared keys, or (b) the genome ends up with two ranges for the same param. Net effect: the genome is NOT actually narrowed/deduped as claimed, and breadth stays as-is or grows. This must be fixed before any evolution run.

GENOME BREADTH UNBOUNDED FOR WIDEST STRATEGIES: vol_regime_arb already has 14 signal-block keys and chaos_trend 12; lstm_pattern 13. After injecting the ~13-key SHARED_LONG_CORE, vol_regime_arb would carry ~25+ evolvable dimensions per genome. The plan's only stated breadth cap is 'max 3 evolvable booleans per strategy' — it imposes NO cap on total continuous dimensions. With pop 60-80 and only 8-12 generations, a 25-dimension genome is under-sampled and overfit-prone on pre-lockbox data. No per-strategy total-key ceiling is specified.

BUILD-HELPER / DEDUP IS UNTESTED-BY-DESIGN AT THE COLLISION POINT: The plan's unit test only asserts 'every registry entry has the full SHARED_LONG_CORE keys and zero forbidden short-only keys.' It does NOT assert the ABSENCE of the now-duplicated inline exec/gate keys (stop_loss_atr_mult, take_profit_atr_mult, adx_min, volume_threshold) in the Layer-B blocks, so the most likely implementation bug (failing to strip inline copies) would pass the test.
recommendations: Add an explicit pipeline step (and document it in iteration 1) where the build-helper DELETES inline exec/gate/size keys (stop_loss_atr_mult, take_profit_atr_mult, time_stop_hours, adx_min, adx_max, volume_threshold, rsi_overbought, rsi_oversold, any vol_target) from every param_space AND from each strategy PARAMS dict, THEN injects SHARED_LONG_CORE. Treat this as a precondition of the merge, not an afterthought.

Strengthen the unit test: assert that NO Layer-B param_space contains any key in the SHARED_LONG_CORE keyset (i.e. zero collisions), in addition to asserting all shared keys are present and all short-only keys are absent. Also assert evolvable-boolean count <= 3 per strategy programmatically.

Impose an explicit total-dimension ceiling per genome (e.g. <= 18 evolvable keys = ~13 shared + <=5-6 signal-block). vol_regime_arb (14 signal keys) and chaos_trend (12) exceed any reasonable budget for pop 60-80 / 8-12 gens; trim their signal blocks (the plan already proposes derived/fixed keys for some — extend that discipline) before re-evolution.

Where total dimensions stay high, raise generations or population for that strategy slice, or freeze additional low-impact keys, so search coverage matches dimensionality. State the dimension/pop/gen relationship explicitly in the per-cluster iteration actions.

Verified-good and worth preserving as-is: (1) lockbox is causal — load_data() uses get_evolution_window() -> in_sample() which drops bars >= cutoff with a hard assert (auto_evolve.py:1493-1497); _regime is computed per-bar from a single row (df.iloc[bar_idx]) using only rolling/causal features, so no cross-lockbox regime leak. Keep lockbox_days=730 change. (2) The 3-arg wrapper at auto_evolve.py:519-523 is the single central choke-point and matches the backtester's strategy_fn(df,i,position) call sites (lines 301,385), so the proposed None-filter for non-long signals is implementable exactly as described. (3) OOS WF test folds carry ZERO selection weight (fitness uses train/val Sharpe only) — preserve this. (4) funding_bps_per_8h default is 1.0 (perp cost, charged on any open position, lines 247-261); setting it to 0.0 for spot is correct and structurally bars funding-as-PnL. Add a regression assert that Phase-3 runs use 0.0.

Confirm warm-start cannot reintroduce lockbox/short-era leakage: _load_seed_genomes resets seed fitness to -999 and re-evaluates on in-sample data (good), but seeds are short-era PARAMS. The plan's 'seed at most ONE genome' rule must be enforced in code (warm_start_max_frac currently caps at 0.5 of pop, auto_evolve.py:943) — lower this cap to 1 genome for Phase-3 or the dedup intent is violated.

testers.focus: Phase-3 preflight: reproducibility (snapshot/manifest/seeds) AND implementability of every proposed change against the repo as-is. Adversarial leakage/long-only audit.
blocking_issues: FROZEN SNAPSHOT IS ONLY 365 DAYS, NOT 2019->2026. benchmark/snapshots/manifest.json: every symbol n_bars=8760, first_ts=2025-06-07, last_ts=2026-06-07. The plan's entire data narrative ('~2019->2026', 'last 2 years sealed lockbox', 'set lockbox_days=730') is NOT implementable on the current snapshot. Setting lockbox_days=730 makes DataLockbox.in_sample() EMPTY: cutoff = last_ts - 730d = 2024-06-08 which is BEFORE first_ts (2025-06-07), so ALL bars fall into the lockbox and zero bars remain for evolution. Verified arithmetically. The plan never lists 'rebuild a multi-year snapshot' as a prerequisite, so iterations 1-8 would either crash on the hard assert (auto_evolve.py:1494) or silently fall back to an UNSEALED full-series live load (auto_evolve.py:1505-1517).

history_days=365 IS HARDCODED in benchmark/spec.py:38 and the snapshot builder fetches since_days=spec.history_days (data_lockbox.py:108). A re-snapshot WITHOUT also raising history_days (to ~2555 for 7y) still yields only 1 year, so lockbox_days=730 still empties in-sample. Both spec fields must change together AND a network re-fetch + `python -m benchmark snapshot --force` must run, which re-hashes all parquet sha256 in the manifest. This is a multi-step data-engineering prerequisite absent from the priority_ranking and iteration_1 plan.

SILENT LEAKAGE FALLBACK: load_data() (auto_evolve.py:1488-1517) catches ANY exception from the lockbox path and falls back to a full-series LIVE load that is explicitly 'NOT lockbox-sealed'. After a re-snapshot, the DataLockbox sha256 integrity check (data_lockbox.py:172-181) REFUSES to run on byte-mismatched parquet and raises; that raise is swallowed and turns into an unsealed full-history load that DOES include 2024-06-07..2026-06-07. The plan's iteration-1 'smoke test to confirm sealed lockbox' does not assert that the lockbox PATH was actually taken (not the fallback). This is a concrete way the lockbox could secretly leak.

CROSS-ASSET IN-SAMPLE FRAMES ARE KEYED ON THE SAME lockbox_days (auto_evolve.py:853-862 and data_lockbox.py:209). lockbox_days=730 makes each cross-asset in_sample frame empty -> the len(in_sample)<200 guard (line 862) silently SKIPS every cross asset -> cross_asset_generalisation term silently disappears from fitness with no error, quietly weakening the anti-overfit objective the plan relies on.

RE-ADMITTING PRUNED STRATEGIES IS HEAVIER THAN STATED. STRATEGY_REGISTRY currently has ONLY 11 active entries; the 10 strategies the plan schedules for iterations 6-8 (momentum, connors_rsi2, vwap_reversion, williams_cci, supertrend, obv_divergence, rsi_divergence, mean_reversion, heikin_ashi_ema, volatility_breakout) are NOT in the registry (verified: only trend_following, donchian_breakout, dual_ma, keltner_breakout, volatility_squeeze, ichimoku_kumo, kama_trend, fisher_transform, chaos_trend, vol_regime_arb, lstm_pattern present). 'Gated re-admission' actually requires re-adding full registry entries + param_space + SHARED_LONG_CORE injection + short-branch surgery per file, not just a code fix + trade gate.
recommendations: BEFORE iteration 1, add an explicit DATA-PREP step: (a) raise benchmark/spec.py history_days to >=2555 (7y) and lockbox_days to 730 TOGETHER; (b) run `python -m benchmark snapshot --force` with network to refetch + re-freeze multi-year parquet (this rewrites all sha256 in manifest.json); (c) assert manifest first_ts<=2019 and lockbox_cutoff_ts == last_ts-730d == ~2024-06-07. Do NOT change lockbox_days against the existing 365d snapshot.

Make the lockbox NON-OPTIONAL for Phase 3: change load_data so that when use_data_lockbox=True a snapshot/integrity failure RAISES instead of falling back to the unsealed live load (auto_evolve.py:1505-1517). The current silent fallback is the single biggest secret-leakage vector. Add a startup assertion that logs and hard-fails if the fallback branch is reached.

In the iteration-1 smoke test, assert POSITIVELY that the sealed path executed: check the log line 'Data lockbox: loaded N in-sample bars' is present, N>0, and df['timestamp'].max() < lockbox_cutoff_ts AND lockbox_cutoff_ts corresponds to 2024-06-07. Also assert cross-asset frames are non-empty (guard against the silent <200 skip).

Confirm the cross-asset in-sample frames are non-empty after lockbox_days=730 (they will be once the 7y snapshot exists). Add an explicit error if cross_paths==[] while cross_asset_generalisation is enabled, so the generalization term cannot silently vanish.

Long-only mechanism is solid and implementable as written (engine flag at backtester.py:384 entry block + the existing signal_exit path at line 302; wrapper at auto_evolve.py:519-523; fitness net via trades_df 'side' column which exists via Trade.to_dict; funding_bps_per_8h=0.0). Keep all five layers. One refinement: also guard the position-management read at line 301 so a short signal vs a long position routes to signal_exit (it already does) and can never be reinterpreted as a new short — verified the current code does this, so just add a unit test asserting zero trades with side!='long' end-to-end.

Add the planned bmsb_macro_bullish to engine/features.py: verified bmsb_sma/bmsb_ema are 20/21-bar (~20h) at features.py:133-140 and 295-296 — confirmed NOT macro. close.rolling(480).mean() (~20d on 1h) is correct and implementable. Ensure the 480-bar warmup is covered by warmup_bars/burn-in so early bars are NaN-safe.

For iterations 6-8, replace 'gated re-admission' wording with explicit registry-entry reconstruction tasks: each pruned strategy needs a new STRATEGY_REGISTRY block (module/function/params_dict/param_space) + SHARED_LONG_CORE merge + short-branch deletion in its file. Budget accordingly; these are medium-effort, not low.

Keep the verified anti-leakage fitness architecture intact: selection uses is_train_sharpe/is_val_sharpe (WF train/val folds inside in-sample) with WF TEST Sharpe at ZERO weight (auto_evolve.py:603-604, comment 582-585). Do not let any refactor introduce wf_agg.sharpe_ratio into the fitness sum.

Re-snapshotting invalidates DSR n_trials provenance and any warm-start seeds tuned on the old 365d/short-era data. Confirm prior_trials/_load_prior_trials and HoF seeds are reset (the plan's 'discard evolved PARAMS as finals, seed at most one genome' is right; extend it to clearing stale DSR trial counts if they were computed on the wrong data window).
